fact: October 16 is exactly 289 days into the year, which means there are only 76 days left until New Year's Eve!
In the Northern Hemisphere, squirrels are frantically burying nuts right now - they can bury up to 10,000 acorns in a single season and forget where half of them are!
The sun is setting almost 2 minutes earlier each day around October 16 in most places, making bedtime feel like it comes super fast.
October 16 falls smack in the middle of Libra season, which means the zodiac scales are perfectly balanced - just like how day and night were almost equal a few weeks ago!
Maple trees are dumping their leaves like crazy right now in cold places, and a single big maple can drop over 600,000 leaves in one autumn!
Why do spiders seem to be everywhere in mid-October? Because this is when house spiders are desperately looking for warm places to spend winter - your house looks pretty cozy to them!
The moon around October 16 can look extra orange and huge when it rises because of all the dust and particles floating in the crisp autumn air.
In Australia and other Southern Hemisphere places, October 16 is prime baby animal season - lambs, calves, and joeys are being born as spring gets warmer!
Morning frost starts appearing on grass around this date in many cold regions, creating millions of tiny ice crystals that melt as soon as the sun touches them.
Geese flying south make their loudest honking sounds in mid-October because they're basically yelling directions to each other during their longest flight days.
October 16 is roughly 25 days after the autumn equinox, which means winter is officially closer than summer now in the Northern Hemisphere.
Apple trees are at their absolute peak ripeness right now - the sugars have concentrated perfectly and the nights are cold enough to make them extra crispy and sweet!
